commit.message:
fix: require SECRET_TOKEN at startup to prevent auth bypass (#476)

* fix: require SECRET_TOKEN at startup to prevent auth bypass

The original verify_token() allowed None == None when SECRET_TOKEN was
unset, silently disabling authentication.  The server now refuses to
start without SECRET_TOKEN configured, and validate_user() accepts the
static token via constant-time comparison (hmac.compare_digest) as an
alternative to DB-backed OAuth tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add pylint disable for wrong-import-position in conftest.py

The imports must come after os.environ.setdefault() for SECRET_TOKEN,
which is intentionally non-standard. Suppress the C0413 warning.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add SECRET_TOKEN to Playwright CI workflow env

The 'Start FastAPI application' step was missing SECRET_TOKEN, causing
the app to crash at startup with RuntimeError since the PR made it
required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement Tab navigation and keyboard focus management

Extends PublicWidget interface with SetFocused/IsFocused methods to enable
keyboard focus management. Implements automatic Tab/Shift+Tab navigation
through focusable widgets (Button, TextInput).

Changes:
- Add SetFocused/IsFocused to PublicWidget interface and BasePublicWidget
- Add Focusable interface with CanTakeFocus method
- Implement focus chain auto-building from widget tree in FocusManager
- Update EventDispatcher.dispatchKey to handle Tab/Shift+Tab navigation
- Add keyboard activation (Enter/Space) for focused Buttons
- Add visual focus indicators (borders) for Button and TextInput
- Remove duplicate focused field from TextInput (use BasePublicWidget's)
- Add comprehensive tests: TestFocusNavigation, TestFocusableInterface,
  TestKeyboardActivation, TestFocusChainAutoBuilding
- Update doc.go with focus management examples

Validation:
- GOOS=windows go build ./... passes
- GOOS=windows go vet ./... passes
- Complexity metrics within acceptable thresholds (dispatchKey: 8)

Resolves AUDIT-2026-03-10.md first finding.

commit.message:
ci: add Claude Code review workflow (#712)

* ci: add Claude Code review workflow with grumpy-review skill

Adds a GitHub Actions workflow that triggers automated code review
when a "claude review" label is applied to a PR. Uses the claudius
grumpy-review and check-pr-comments skills via MCP-first approach.

- Triggers on label application and subsequent pushes
- Checks/resolves previously addressed review threads
- Approves and removes label when all comments resolved
- Falls back to opus model when CLAUDE_MODEL var is not set
- Skips gracefully when OAuth token is not configured

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Workflow name: "Claude (label: claudius-review)"
- Uses --agent claudius:claudius to run as Claudius directly
- Persona instructions in prompt for PR comment tone

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: fix permissions, reorder review flow, post MEDIUM+ only

- Add issues: write permission for label removal (CMT-001)
- Reorder flow: check existing → resolve fixed → fresh review → post MEDIUM+ → remove label → approve if clean
- Always remove claudius-review label (human-triggered each time)
- Approve only when no unresolved comments remain after full flow

Label removal was inside the Claude prompt, so timeouts or failures
would leave it stuck and retrigger on every push. Now handled by a
dedicated step with if: always().

Keep claudius-review label on failure/timeout so subsequent pushes
retrigger the review automatically until it completes successfully.

commit.message:
fix: resolve summarize parse failures and replace Playwright with Bright Data

ai_client.py:
- Add _THINKING_DISABLE_EXTRA_BODY mapping to disable thinking mode for
  NVIDIA thinking models (kimi-k2.5, qwen3 variants) that return empty
  content and prose in reasoning_content, breaking JSON parsing
- Pass extra_body in _request_completion for matched NVIDIA models
- Add _extract_last_json_block() to recover JSON from reasoning_content
  as a belt-and-suspenders fallback after thinking-mode disable
- Replace blind reasoning_content passthrough with proper JSON extraction;
  raise ValueError (skip provider) if no JSON block found

summarize.py:
- Add _BLOCKED_CONTENT_PATTERNS frozenset (Access Denied, Cloudflare, etc.)
- Extend _validate_content_integrity to detect bot-detection/error pages
  before wasting an LLM call on scraped access-denied content

scrape_articles.py:
- Replace Playwright with requests + Bright Data Web Unlocker REST API
  (POST https://api.brightdata.com/request, Bearer BRIGHTDATA_API_KEY)
- Use BeautifulSoup/lxml for HTML-to-text extraction (already in deps)
- Plain requests fallback when BRIGHTDATA_API_KEY is not set
- BRIGHTDATA_ZONE env var configures zone (default: web_unlocker1)

requirements.txt:

commit.message:
feat: VM snapshot/restore for fast cold start (#8)

* feat: VM snapshot/restore with SnapshotManager and integration tests

Add snapshot/restore support using Cloud Hypervisor's native API for
fast VM cold start (~60ms restore vs ~460ms boot).

Core additions:
- SnapshotManager (snapshot.rs): golden snapshot lifecycle
  - ensure_golden_snapshot(): boot minimal VM, verify agent, snapshot
  - restore_vm(): per-instance config.json path rewriting + symlinked
    memory file (avoids copying 128MB per restore)
  - Lazy creation on first use, reused across all restores

- VmManager extensions (vm.rs):
  - create_and_boot_vm_for_snapshot(): boots without virtiofs to avoid
    vhost-user protocol state issue that blocks restore
  - from_restored(): wraps a restored VM into VmManager
  - add_net()/add_net_to_socket(): hot-add virtio-net post-restore
  - ch_pid(): accessor for process management
  - Cleaned up restore()/receive_migration() to use api_request_to_socket
    instead of raw HTTP with inconsistent response parsing

Integration tests (3 new):
- test_snapshot_and_restore: basic snapshot/restore cycle
- test_snapshot_manager_golden_lifecycle: golden create + 2 concurrent restores
- test_snapshot_restore_with_networking: restore + net hot-add

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: integrate snapshot restore into VM pool warming

Wire SnapshotManager into VmPool so pool warming uses snapshot restore
(~60ms) instead of cold boot (~460ms) when a golden snapshot exists.
Falls back to full boot transparently if no snapshot is available.

- VmPool now owns a SnapshotManager
- warm() creates golden snapshot lazily on first use
- create_warm_vm() tries snapshot restore, falls back to cold boot
- snapshot_manager() exposes the manager for external use

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix node stall from fork detection busy-loop

- Require >=2 peers on alternate chain before triggering a same-height
  fork switch (prevents single minority peer from becoming 'consensus')
- Add 30-second cooldown between same-height fork switch attempts for
  the same (height, hash) pair (prevents tight spin when sync_from_peers
  returns immediately with no peers ahead)
- Add 5-second sleep in main.rs after same-height fork sync paths so the
  tokio runtime is not starved by a tight loop
- Clear stale FetchingChain fork_state when the 2-peer guard fires, so
  future legitimate fork resolutions are not blocked
- Fix sync loop detection to return Ok(None) instead of an empty
  BlocksResponse; an empty response is indistinguishable from 'no blocks
  in range' and causes the requesting peer to retry immediately

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(mc,hud): eliminate phantom surface noise margin + fix altimeter

Two fixes:

1. compute_surface_cache all-solid margin (chunk_loader.rs)
   The previous margin was max_v.y + 0.5 but fbm noise amplitude is
   up to ±0.52, so density at the top grid point could be near-zero
   or slightly negative, causing MC to find a zero-crossing and draw
   a thin phantom triangle there (the remaining green cloud /
   horizontal ledge inside cliffs).  Increased margin to max_v.y + 2.0
   (4× the noise amplitude) — density at the top is now always ≥ 1.48,
   guaranteeing no zero-crossing regardless of noise.

2. Altimeter (metaworld_alpha.rs)
   The terrain formula encodes geographic altitude as:
     surface_voxel_y = origin_voxel.y + (geo_alt − origin_alt)
   so local.y == altitude offset from origin exactly.
   player.position.to_gps().alt was wrong: ECEF Y at Brisbane is only
   ~0.40× the geographic-up direction, so the displayed altitude was
   ~60 % of the true value AND drifted with horizontal movement (hence
   'numbers loosely based on a combination of x,y,z').
   Fixed to: origin_gps.alt + physics.ecef_to_local(&player.position).y

Bump: v0.1.72 → v0.1.73

commit.message:
Fused matmul+ReLU kernel and B=48: 23.93 TFLOPS (95.7% peak)

Two optimizations that nearly saturate the hardware:

1. Fused C = ReLU(A × B) kernel (matmul_relu.cc):
   - Replaces 3 separate calls (zero + matmul + relu) with 1 fused call
   - Zero-initializes accumulators in registers (no memory round-trip)
   - Applies ReLU during store phase (no separate load/store pass)
   - Reduces hardware loop body from 6 to 2 kernel dispatches

2. Increased batch size B=16 → B=48:
   - Matmul outer loop goes from 1 to 3 iterations (M/(2r) = 48/16 = 3)
   - Much better SIMD pipeline utilization
   - SRAM: 32KB (W) + 12KB×2 (act) + 1KB (stack) = 57KB < 64KB

Combined result: 8.98 → 23.93 TFLOPS (2.67× improvement)
Per-tile: 374 → 997 GFLOPS (2.67× improvement)
CPU speedup: 18.5× → 25.9×
